markdown:
#  Which budget views should I add to my Procore projects? 
##  Answer 
In addition to your Procore Standard Budget view, Procore recommends setting up the following budget views for your projects:
  * Buyout Savings
  * Buyout Savings (While in Buyout)
  * Change Order Analysis
  * Current/Revised
  * Real-Time Labor Costing
  * Procore Labor Productivity Cost


#####  Tip
If your company is using Procore's Resource Tracking tools, you can also set up a budget view to perform real-time labor costing. See [Add a Budget View for Real-Time Labor Costing](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/resource-tracking-and-project-financials-setup-guide/).
### Buyout Savings
If the project buyout phase is complete, the 'Buyout Savings' budget view shows your project's buyout savings. _Project buyout_ refers to the time period between preconstruction and construction phases of a project when commitments (a.k.a., purchase orders and subcontracts) are issued. _Buyout savings_ refers to situations where the work's estimated costs are higher than the actual market price, with the buyout savings being posted and held in a contingency fund. Typically, project buyout occurs after a contract award to a general contractor and that contractor's awarding of subcontracts. You can use the 'Buyout Savings' view to filter your project's budget by cost type to show only the budget line items to be committed. See [Apply the View, Snapshot, Group, and Filter Options on a Budget View](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/budget-project/tutorials/apply-the-view-snapshot-group-and-filter-options-on-a-budget-view).
_Note_ : If the buyout is incomplete on a line item, the 'Committed Costs' amount in the view will be reflected as buyout savings. See Buyout Savings (While in Buyout).
  1. Navigate to the company's **Admin** tool.
  2. Under **Project Settings** , click **Budget**.
  3. Click **Set up New Budget View**.
  4. Under **Standard Views** , highlight **Procore Standard Budget**.
  5. Click **Create**.
  6. Type the following in the **View Name** box: `Buyout Savings`
  7. Type a description for your new view in the **View Description** box.
  8. Under **Column Configuration** , click **Configure Columns.**
  9. Ensure that all the default column checkboxes remain selected
  10. Modify the **Committed Costs** column as follows:
    1. Under **Source** , highlight **Committed Costs**.
    2. Click **Edit**.
    3. Remove the mark from the **Commitment Change Orders** checkbox.
    4. Click **Save as New** or **Update**.
  11. Create a **Buyout Savings** column as follows:
    1. Click **Create > Calculated**.
    2. Type the following in the **Column Name** box: Buyout Savings
    3. In the **Format** list, ensure _Currency_ is selected.
    4. In the Pick a Column list, select:
       * _Original Budget_
       * _minus (-)_
       * _Committed Costs_
    5. Click **Create**.
  12. In the column list, remove the mark from all column checkboxes.
  13. Place a mark in the following column checkboxes only:
     * **Sub Job**
     * **Division**
     * **Cost Code**
     * **Category**
     * **Original Budget**
     * **Committed Costs**
     * **Buyout Savings**  

  14. Click the (x) to close the Configure Columns window.  
  
![Forrealnewbuyout](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/1932a5aaae238aa51f1866bd07ee82d5/f6cb6136d2bd1564ceae98998d430cdd/forrealnewbuyout.png?fm=webp&h=334)


### Buyout Savings (While in Buyout)
You can use this budget view instead of the one above if buyout is not complete on certain line items.
  1. Navigate to the company's **Admin** tool.
  2. Under **Project Settings** , click **Budget**.
  3. Click **Set up New Budget View**.
  4. Under **Standard Views** , highlight **Procore Standard Budget**.
  5. Click **Create**.
  6. Type the following in the **View Name** box: `Buyout Savings`
  7. Type a description for your new view in the **View Description** box.
  8. Under **Column Configuration** , click **Configure Columns.**
  9. Ensure that all the default column checkboxes remain selected
  10. Modify the **Committed Costs** column as follows:
    1. Under **Source** , highlight **Committed Costs**.
    2. Click **Edit**.
    3. Remove the checkmark from the **Commitment Change Orders** checkbox.
    4. Click **Save as New** or **Update**.
  11. Create a **Buyout Savings Intermediate** column as follows:
    1. Click **Create > Calculated**.
    2. Type the following in the **Column Name** box: Buyout Savings Intermediate
    3. In the **Format** list, ensure _Currency_ is selected.
    4. In the Pick a Column list, select:
       * _Original Budget_
       * _minus (-)_
       * _Committed Costs_
    5. Click **Create**.
  12. Create a **Buyout Savings** column as follows:
  13. Click **Create > Calculated**.
  14. Type the following in the **Column Name** box: `Buyout Savings`
  15. In the **Format** list, ensure _Currency_ is selected.
  16. In the **Pick a Column** list, select:
     * _(Buyout Savings Intermediate_
     * _multiplied by (*)_
     * _Committed Costs)_
     * divided by (÷)
     * _Committed Costs_  
 _  
Notes_ : 
       * If the Committed Cost is equal to zero ($0), Procore shows the **Buyout Savings** value as 'N/A.'
       * If the Committed Cost is not equal to $0, Procore shows the **Buyout Savings**.
  17. Click **Create**.
  18. In the column list, remove the checkmark from all column checkboxes.
  19. Place a checkmark in the following boxes only:
     * **Buyout Savings**
     * **Original Budget Amount**
     * **Committed Costs**
  20. Click the (x) to close the Configure Columns window.  
  
![Originalbuyout](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/b4a3a4f2fd98d84d93feb29656bfef72/956a2e66f6ba6dd6b41dc6bd9a01dd69/originalbuyout.png?fm=webp&h=196)


### Change Order Analysis
You can compare how much money you're making/losing off Out-of-Scope change orders, and how much money you're losing off of In-Scope change orders._Note_ : This view only works if you are using the Change Events tool.
  1. Navigate to the company's **Admin** tool.
  2. Under **Project Settings** , click **Budget**.
  3. Click **Set up New Budget View**.
  4. Under **Standard Views** , highlight **Procore Standard Budget**.
  5. Click **Create**.
  6. Title the view _Change Order Analysis_.
  7. Click **Configure Columns**.
  8. Rename the **Approved COs** column
    1. Click into **Approved COs** and click **Edit.**
    2. Name the column _Approved Prime COs_.
    3. Click **Update**.
  9. Create the **Approved CCOs - Out of Scope** column.
    1. Click **Create** in the top right corner and select **Source**.
    2. Name the column _Approved CCOs - Out of Scope_.
    3. Select **Commitment** as the column source.
    4. Select the checkbox Commitment Change Orders.
    5. Select the box next to "Approved" under Status.
    6. Select the checkbox next to "Out of Scope."
    7. Click **Create** at the bottom of the module.
  10. Create the **Approved CCOs - In Scope** column.
    1. Click **Create** in the top right corner and select **Source**.
    2. Name the column _Approved CCOs - In Scope_.
    3. Select **Commitment** as the column source.
    4. Select the checkbox next to Commitment Change Orders.
    5. Select the checkbox next to "Approved" under Status.
    6. Select the checkbox next to "In Scope."
    7. Click **Create** at the bottom of the module.
  11. Create the **Out of Scope CO Variance** column.
    1. Click **Create** in the upper right corner and select **Calculated**.
    2. Title the column **Out of Scope CO Variance**.
    3. Enter the following calculation: **Approved (Prime) COs - Approved CCOs-Out of Scope.**
    4. Click **Create** at the bottom of the module.
  12. Create the **Total Variance** column.
    1. Click **Create** in the top right corner and select **Calculated Column**.
    2. Title the column _Total Variance._
    3. Enter the following calculation: Out of Scope CO Variance - Approved CCOs In Scope.
    4. Click **Create** at the bottom of the modal.
  13. Remove the checkmark from _all_ column boxes except:
    1. Sub Job
    2. Division
    3. Cost Code
    4. Category
    5. The columns that you just created.
  14. Click the (x) to close the Configure Columns window.  
  
![Change Order Analysis New](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/fb4fbdb66201b6f98a6ca5b4624de943/aa86b92be25107d6e7a5d3e0db5ca777/change-order-analysis-new.png?fm=webp&h=233)


### Current/Revised
This is similar to the "normal" budget; however, all Pending, Projected, and Forecast columns are removed. The Over/Under for this view will show you the over/under for current amounts.
  1. Navigate to the company's **Admin** tool.
  2. Under **Project Settings** , click **Budget**.
  3. Click **Set up New Budget View**.
  4. Under **Standard Views** , highlight **Procore Standard Budget**.
  5. Click **Create**.
  6. Title the view _Current/Revised_.
  7. Click **Configure Columns**.
  8. Create the **Current Over/Under** column.
    1. Click **Create** in the top right corner and select **Calculated** column.
    2. Name the column _Current Over/Under_.
    3. Enter the calculation Revised Budget - Committed Costs - Direct Costs.
    4. Click **Create** at the bottom of the module.
  9. Deselect the following columns in the left.
     * Pending Cost Changes
     * Pending Budget Changes
     * Projected Costs
     * Projected Budget
     * Projected over Under
     * Forecast to Complete
  10. Exit the window to save your changes.  
  



#### Real-Time Labor Costing
For companies using Procore's Resource Tracking tools and who want to use Procore's real-time labor costing capabilities in the Budget tool, Procore recommends setting up a budget view named 'Real-Time Labor Productivity' and adding the following columns. For step-by-step instructions about setting up the individual columns, see [Add a Real-Time Labor Productivity Budget View](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/admin-company/tutorials/add-a-real-time-labor-productivity-budget-view) or [Resource Tracking and Project Financials: Setup Guide](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/resource-tracking-and-project-financials-setup-guide/).
![Budget View Rtlc](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/629ca95b5974b5eda91e3e57293c7bef/bf5166d8d8815befdc9e9e08f267002b/budget-view-rtlc.png?fm=webp)
#### Procore Labor Productivity Cost
For companies using Procore's Project Financials and Resource Tracking tools, Procore recommends setting up a budget view named 'Procore Labor Productivity Cost' budget view. This is a pre-built view that you can quickly add to a project. It allows users to input production quantities (see [What are production quantities?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-production-quantities)) to your project's budget. For instructions, see [Set Up the Procore Labor Productivity Cost Budget View](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/admin-company/tutorials/set-up-the-procore-labor-productivity-cost-budget-view).
![Labor Cost Productivity View](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/396daa1a5c46eba6d3c449c4ec6a6274/1671d391882cdd755571e5801791d862/labor-cost-productivity-view.png?fm=webp)

title: Which budget views should I add to my Procore projects?
description: This FAQ provides a summary of recommended budget views to consider adding to your Procore project.
